etymology.description: Depicts a man with the neck in shackles (original form of 殃 (殃) (yāng, "misfortune")) -> later borrowed for 'center'
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted a man in shackles, which became the basis for its use to mean 'misfortune' in 殃(殃) (yāng, "misfortune"). It was then borrowed purely for its sound to represent the concept 'center', thus losing any direct pictographic link to its current meaning.

examples.chinese: 我们住在城市的中央(我們住在城市的中央)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhù zài chéngshì de zhōngyāng.
examples.english: We live in the center of the city.

examples.chinese: 游戏的重点是抢占足球场中央(遊戲的重點是搶占足球場中央)。
examples.pinyin: Yóuxì de zhòngdiǎn shì qiǎngzhàn zúqiúchǎng zhōngyāng.
examples.english: The focus of the game is to occupy the center of the soccer field.

examples.chinese: 灯悬挂在客厅的中央(燈懸掛在客廳的中央)。
examples.pinyin: Dēng xuánguà zài kètīng de zhōngyāng.
examples.english: The lamp is hanging in the center of the living room.
